<content>1: # Spec: 260313-auto-965
2: 
3: ## Overview
4: 
5: Configure Graph Display Range (X and Y) in Admin Graph Editor. Enable content authors (admins) to manually control the visible display area (X and Y axes) of graphs within the Admin Graph Editor, ensuring students see a specifically framed portion of the graph.
6: 
7: ## Requirements
8: 
9: ### FR-001: Automatic Range Calculation by Default
10: 
11: **Priority**: MUST
12: **Description**: Graphs must automatically calculate and display their visible range based on the plotted content by default. The system should analyze all graph functions, points, and elements to determine an appropriate viewport that includes all content with reasonable padding.
13: 
14: ### FR-002: Manual Range Override Mode
15: 
16: **Priority**: MUST
17: **Description**: Authors must have the ability to override the automatic behavior and switch to a manual range configuration via a toggle/button control in the AxisConfigPanel.
18: 
19: ### FR-003: Manual Range Input Fields
20: 
21: **Priority**: MUST
22: **Description**: When manual mode is active, authors must be able to specify the minimum and maximum visible boundaries for both the X-axis and the Y-axis using input fields:
23: - xMin: Minimum visible X value
24: - xMax: Maximum visible X value
25: - yMin: Minimum visible Y value
26: - yMax: Maximum visible Y value
27: 
28: ### FR-004: Min-Max Validation
29: 
30: **Priority**: MUST
31: **Description**: The system must enforce that the defined minimum value is always strictly less than the maximum value for each respective axis. Display validation error if xMin >= xMax or yMin >= yMax.
32: 
33: ### FR-005: Numeric Value Validation
34: 
35: **Priority**: MUST
36: **Description**: Only numerical values (including negatives and decimals) are permitted. The input fields should accept numeric values and display an error for non-numeric input.
37: 
38: ### FR-006: Empty Grid Warning
39: 
40: **Priority**: SHOULD
41: **Description**: If the manually configured range completely excludes the drawn graph/function (i.e., all graph y-values fall outside the visible y-range), the system must display a warning to the author. This should be a non-blocking warning that allows saving but alerts the author.
42: 
43: ### FR-007: Frontend Rendering Integration
44: 
45: **Priority**: MUST
46: **Description**: The manually configured limits must be saved with the graph and accurately reflect the visible area when rendered for students in exercises, lessons, or exams. Students do not have access to alter these display ranges. The frontend JSXGraphBoard component must use the configured viewport values.
47: 
48: ### FR-008: Viewport Schema Extension
49: 
50: **Priority**: MUST
51: **Description**: Extend the AxisSpecV1 schema to include a viewportMode field ('auto' | 'manual') and ensure backward compatibility with existing graphs that don't have this field (default to 'auto').
52: 
53: ## Acceptance Criteria
54: 
55: - [ ] Graph range is automatic by default (viewportMode defaults to 'auto')
56: - [ ] Admin user can switch to manual configuration mode via toggle
57: - [ ] Admin user can input X-min, X-max, Y-min, Y-max values when in manual mode
58: - [ ] System validates that Min < Max for both axes (displays error)
59: - [ ] System validates that inputs are numeric (displays error)
60: - [ ] System displays a warning if the configured range results in an empty grid (excludes the function)
61: - [ ] The manually set range correctly controls the visible graph area in the admin preview
62: - [ ] Settings are saved and correctly rendered on the student-facing frontend
63: 
64: ## Guardrails
65: 
66: - **Backward Compatibility**: Existing graphs without viewportMode should default to automatic calculation
67: - **Data Integrity**: The viewport values must be stored in the existing AxisSpecV1 structure under the viewport field
68: - **No Breaking Changes**: The frontend rendering must continue to work for graphs that don't have manual viewport settings
69: 
70: ## Out of Scope
71: 
72: - Automatic viewport calculation algorithm details (the specific algorithm for calculating auto-viewport is implementation-specific)
73: - Real-time auto-update while editing graphs (manual refresh is acceptable)
74: - Zoom/pan functionality for end-users (students)
75: - Saving multiple viewport presets
76: 
77: ## Open Questions
78: 
79: - Should the empty grid warning be a validation error (blocking save) or a warning (allowing save)? Based on the task description, it should be a warning (non-blocking).

(End of file - total 79 lines)
</content>

**Priority**: MUST
**Description**: Authors must have the ability to override the automatic behavior and switch to a manual range configuration via a toggle/button control in the AxisConfigPanel.

### FR-003: Manual Range Input Fields

53: /**
54:  * Calculate the auto viewport based on graph elements
55:  * Analyzes points, graphs, asymptotes, and line segments to determine a bounding box
56:  */
57: export function calculateAutoViewport(spec: AxisSpecV1): ViewportBounds {
58:   const DEFAULT_BOUNDS: ViewportBounds = {
59:     xMin: -10,
60:     xMax: 10,
61:     yMin: -10,
62:     yMax: 10,
63:   }
64: 
65:   // Start with default bounds
66:   let xMin = DEFAULT_BOUNDS.xMin
67:   let xMax = DEFAULT_BOUNDS.xMax
68:   let yMin = DEFAULT_BOUNDS.yMin
69:   let yMax = DEFAULT_BOUNDS.yMax
70: 
71:   let hasContent = false
72: 
73:   // Analyze points
74:   for (const point of spec.elements.points) {
75:     hasContent = true
76:     xMin = Math.min(xMin, point.x)
77:     xMax = Math.max(xMax, point.x)
78:     yMin = Math.min(yMin, point.y)
79:     yMax = Math.max(yMax, point.y)
80:   }
81: 
82:   // Analyze graphs (sample at multiple x values)
83:   const sampleCount = 20
84:   const sampleRange = 10 // Sample from -10 to 10
85: 
86:   for (const graph of spec.elements.graphs) {
87:     hasContent = true
88:     for (let i = 0; i <= sampleCount; i++) {
89:       const x = -sampleRange + (i / sampleCount) * (sampleRange * 2)
90:       const y = evaluateMathExpression(graph.fn, x)
91:       if (y !== null) {
92:         xMin = Math.min(xMin, x)
93:         xMax = Math.max(xMax, x)
94:         yMin = Math.min(yMin, y)
95:         yMax = Math.max(yMax, y)
96:       }
97:     }
98:   }
99: 
100:   // Analyze vertical asymptotes
101:   for (const xVal of spec.elements.asymptotesVertical || []) {
102:     hasContent = true
103:     xMin = Math.min(xMin, xVal)
104:     xMax = Math.max(xMax, xVal)
105:   }
106: 
107:   // Analyze horizontal asymptotes
108:   for (const yVal of spec.elements.asymptotesHorizontal || []) {
109:     hasContent = true
110:     yMin = Math.min(yMin, yVal)
111:     yMax = Math.max(yMax, yVal)
112:   }
113: 
114:   // Analyze line segments between points
115:   for (const line of spec.elements.lineBetweenPoints || []) {
116:     hasContent = true
117:     xMin = Math.min(xMin, line.a.x, line.b.x)
118:     xMax = Math.max(xMax, line.a.x, line.b.x)
119:     yMin = Math.min(yMin, line.a.y, line.b.y)
120:     yMax = Math.max(yMax, line.a.y, line.b.y)
121:   }
122: 
123:   // If no content, return default bounds
124:   if (!hasContent) {
125:     return DEFAULT_BOUNDS
126:   }
127: 
128:   // Add 10% padding (minimum 1 unit)
129:   const xPadding = Math.max(1, (xMax - xMin) * 0.1)
130:   const yPadding = Math.max(1, (yMax - yMin) * 0.1)
131: 
132:   return {
133:     xMin: xMin - xPadding,
